methods.description: This method involves gathering publications that reported pollination experiments in animal-pollinated crops through a systematic review using databases such as Web of Science, Scopus, and Google Scholar. The focus was on pollination experiments in agricultural contexts with assessments of production levels after natural pollination and hand supplementation treatments.

steps.description: Conduct a general search on Web of Science, Scopus, and Google Scholar with specific search strings.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform a systematic literature search using specified search strings on multiple databases.

steps.description: Search for each selected crop on Web of Science and Google Scholar with specific search strings.
steps.searchable_summary: Conduct crop-specific literature searches using specified search strings.

steps.description: Sieve through 30,961 records based on title and abstract reading for eligibility.
steps.searchable_summary: Filter records based on title and abstract for eligibility.

steps.description: Evaluate 604 studies through full-text reading and review to retrieve data for the dataset.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform full-text review to extract data for the dataset.

steps.description: Extract data for each pollination experiment, including crop production values and response variables.
steps.searchable_summary: Extract crop production values and response variables for each experiment.

steps.description: Estimate standard deviation from other variables when not provided.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate standard deviation from other variables if not provided.

steps.description: Extract values from graphical data using ImageJ.
steps.searchable_summary: Use ImageJ to extract data from graphical representations.

steps.description: Collect geographical and methodological information related to pollination experiments.
steps.searchable_summary: Gather geographical and methodological details for each experiment.
